title: Using Polls in a Microsoft Teams meeting
what_it_is: Polls is a built-in app in Microsoft Teams that lets you ask a live multiple-choice question during a Teams meeting or class, with results shown as they come in.
why_use_it: It gives you a quick, anonymous check of understanding during an online or hybrid lesson, without learners needing a separate app or a login — everything happens inside the Teams meeting they're already in.
how_to: During a Teams meeting, select Apps in the meeting toolbar at the top.
Type your question and add the answer options.
Turn on Keep responses anonymous if you don't want answers linked to individual names.
Select Save, then Launch to send the poll to everyone in the meeting.
Watch results appear live as learners respond, and select End poll when you're ready to share the results with the class.
